sections.title: Physics
sections.description: Hard and Very Hard NEET Physics ‚ÄĒ Rotational Motion, Modern Physics, Optics, Electromagnetism, AC Circuits.

questions.text: A disc of mass 2 kg and radius 0.2 m is rotating with angular velocity 30 rad/s. A constant retarding torque of 0.4 N¬∑m acts on it. The number of revolutions made before it comes to rest is:
questions.explanation: I = ¬ĹMR¬≤ = ¬Ĺ √ó 2 √ó (0.2)¬≤ = 0.04 kg¬∑m¬≤. Angular deceleration őĪ = ŌĄ/I = 0.4/0.04 = 10 rad/s¬≤. Using ŌČ¬≤ = ŌČ‚āÄ¬≤ ‚ąí 2őĪőł: 0 = 900 ‚ąí 20őł ‚Üí őł = 45 rad. Revolutions = őł / 2ŌÄ = 45/2ŌÄ.

questions.text: In a series LCR circuit the resonance frequency is f‚āÄ. If the inductance L and capacitance C are both doubled, the new resonance frequency becomes:
questions.explanation: Resonance frequency f‚āÄ = 1/(2ŌÄ‚ąöLC). When L ‚Üí 2L and C ‚Üí 2C: f' = 1/(2ŌÄ‚ąö(2L √ó 2C)) = 1/(2ŌÄ √ó 2‚ąöLC) = f‚āÄ/2.

questions.text: The de Broglie wavelength of an electron in the second Bohr orbit of hydrogen atom is (a‚āÄ = 0.529 √Ö):
questions.explanation: Bohr's condition: 2ŌÄr‚āô = nőĽ. For n = 2: őĽ = 2ŌÄr‚āā/2 = ŌÄr‚āā. Since r‚āā = n¬≤a‚āÄ = 4a‚āÄ = 4 √ó 0.529 √Ö, we get őĽ = ŌÄ √ó 4 √ó 0.529 = 4ŌÄ √ó 0.529 √Ö ‚Čą 6.64 √Ö.

questions.text: In the photoelectric effect, the frequency of incident radiation is reduced to half the threshold frequency and the intensity is increased four times. The number of photoelectrons emitted per second will:
questions.explanation: Photoelectric emission depends on frequency of incident light, NOT intensity. If the frequency is below the threshold frequency, no photoelectrons are emitted regardless of how high the intensity is. Increasing intensity only increases the number of photons, but each photon still lacks enough energy to eject an electron.

questions.text: A proton and an őĪ-particle are accelerated through the same potential difference V. The ratio of their de Broglie wavelengths őĽ_p : őĽ_őĪ is:
questions.explanation: őĽ = h/p = h/‚ąö(2mqV). őĽ_p/őĽ_őĪ = ‚ąö(m_őĪ ¬∑ q_őĪ) / ‚ąö(m_p ¬∑ q_p) = ‚ąö(4m_p √ó 2e) / ‚ąö(m_p √ó e) = ‚ąö(8m_p¬∑e / m_p¬∑e) = ‚ąö8 = 2‚ąö2. So őĽ_p : őĽ_őĪ = 2‚ąö2 : 1.

questions.text: A thin convex lens of glass (őľ_glass = 1.5) has focal length 20 cm in air. When completely immersed in water (őľ_water = 4/3), its new focal length becomes:

A circular current-carrying loop of radius R lies in the x-y plane with its centre at the origin. A very long straight wire carrying current I is placed along the z-axis. The mutual inductance between the loop and the wire is:
questions.explanation: The magnetic field due to a long straight wire along the z-axis is in the azimuthal (ŌÜ) direction at every point, lying entirely in the x-y plane. The area vector of the circular loop is along the z-axis. Since B is perpendicular to the area vector at every point, the total magnetic flux through the loop is zero. Therefore mutual inductance M = ő¶/I = 0.

sections.title: Chemistry
sections.description: Hard and Very Hard NEET Chemistry ‚ÄĒ Chemical Kinetics, Coordination Compounds, Organic Chemistry, Electrochemistry, Solutions.

questions.text: The rate of a chemical reaction triples when temperature is raised from 20¬įC to 50¬įC. The activation energy of the reaction is approximately: (R = 8.314 J mol‚ĀĽ¬Ļ K‚ĀĽ¬Ļ, ln 3 = 1.099)
questions.explanation: Using Arrhenius: ln(k‚āā/k‚āĀ) = (Ea/R)(1/T‚āĀ ‚ąí 1/T‚āā). T‚āĀ = 293 K, T‚āā = 323 K. 1/T‚āĀ ‚ąí 1/T‚āā = 30/(293 √ó 323) = 3.17 √ó 10‚ĀĽ‚Āī K‚ĀĽ¬Ļ. Ea = 1.099 √ó 8.314 / 3.17 √ó 10‚ĀĽ‚Āī = 9.138 / 3.17 √ó 10‚ĀĽ‚Āī ‚Čą 28,820 J/mol ‚Čą 28.8 kJ/mol.

questions.text: The spin-only magnetic moment of the complex [MnBr‚āĄ]¬≤‚ĀĽ is:
questions.explanation: Mn¬≤‚Āļ has electronic configuration [Ar]3d‚ĀĶ. Br‚ĀĽ is a weak field ligand (does not cause pairing), so all 5 d-electrons remain unpaired. őľ = ‚ąö(n(n+2)) BM = ‚ąö(5 √ó 7) = ‚ąö35 ‚Čą 5.92 BM.

questions.text: When ethanol is heated with excess concentrated H‚āāSO‚āĄ at 170¬įC, the major product formed is:
questions.explanation: At 170¬įC, concentrated H‚āāSO‚āĄ causes intramolecular dehydration (E2 elimination) of ethanol to produce ethene. At 140¬įC, intermolecular dehydration occurs giving diethyl ether. High temperature favours the alkene (Saytzeff product).

questions.text: The pH of 0.1 M acetic acid solution at 25¬įC is: (Ka = 1.8 √ó 10‚ĀĽ‚ĀĶ, log 1.34 = 0.127)
The major product formed in the dehydrohalogenation of 2-bromobutane with alcoholic KOH, according to Saytzeff's rule, is:
questions.explanation: Saytzeff's rule states the more substituted (more stable) alkene is the major product in elimination reactions. 2-Bromobutane can eliminate HBr to give either but-1-ene or but-2-ene. But-2-ene (internal, disubstituted) is more stable due to hyperconjugation and is the Saytzeff product.

questions.text: For the electrochemical cell Zn | ZnSO‚āĄ (0.1M) || CuSO‚āĄ (0.01M) | Cu, the EMF at 25¬įC is: (E¬įcell = 1.10 V, 0.0591/2 = 0.02955, log 10 = 1)
questions.explanation: Nernst equation: E = E¬į ‚ąí (0.0591/n) log(Q). Cell reaction: Zn + Cu¬≤‚Āļ ‚Üí Zn¬≤‚Āļ + Cu. Q = [Zn¬≤‚Āļ]/[Cu¬≤‚Āļ] = 0.1/0.01 = 10. E = 1.10 ‚ąí (0.0591/2) log 10 = 1.10 ‚ąí 0.02955 √ó 1 = 1.07 V.

questions.text: The van't Hoff factor (i) for 0.1 M Ba(NO‚āÉ)‚āā solution that is 80% ionised is:
questions.explanation: Ba(NO‚āÉ)‚āā dissociates into 3 ions: Ba¬≤‚Āļ + 2NO‚āÉ‚ĀĽ (n = 3). For degree of dissociation őĪ = 0.80: i = 1 + őĪ(n ‚ąí 1) = 1 + 0.8(3 ‚ąí 1) = 1 + 0.8 √ó 2 = 1 + 1.6 = 2.6.

sections.title: Biology
sections.description: Hard and Very Hard NEET Biology ‚ÄĒ Genetics, Molecular Biology, Ecology, Evolution, Biotechnology.

questions.text: In a cross AaBbCc √ó AaBbCc (genes on different chromosomes), the probability of obtaining aabbcc offspring is:
questions.explanation: For each independently assorting gene pair Aa √ó Aa, probability of homozygous recessive (aa) = 1/4. With three independent pairs: P(aabbcc) = (1/4) √ó (1/4) √ó (1/4) = 1/64.

questions.text: In a population in Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium, the frequency of homozygous recessive individuals (aa) is 0.09. The frequency of heterozygous individuals (Aa) is:
questions.explanation: q¬≤ = 0.09 ‚Üí q (frequency of 'a') = 0.3. p (frequency of 'A') = 1 ‚ąí 0.3 = 0.7. Heterozygous frequency = 2pq = 2 √ó 0.7 √ó 0.3 = 0.42.

questions.text: Post-transcriptional processing of hnRNA in eukaryotes involves all of the following EXCEPT:

options.text: Addition of 7-methylguanosine cap at the 5' end
Addition of poly-A tail at the 3' end
Addition of poly-A tail at the 5' end

questions.explanation: hnRNA undergoes: (1) 5' capping ‚ÄĒ addition of 7-methylguanosine at 5' end, (2) 3' polyadenylation ‚ÄĒ poly-A tail added at the 3' end (NOT 5' end), and (3) splicing ‚ÄĒ removal of introns. Option D is incorrect because the poly-A tail is always added at the 3' end.

questions.text: A man of blood group A (genotype IAi) marries a woman of blood group B (genotype IBi). The probability that their child will have blood group AB is:
questions.explanation: Cross IAi √ó IBi gives: IAIB (group AB) : IAi (group A) : IBi (group B) : ii (group O) in 1:1:1:1 ratio. Each outcome has probability 1/4. Therefore, probability of AB = 1/4.

questions.text: In a diploid organism (2n = 24), non-disjunction of one chromosome pair occurs during Meiosis II. If the abnormal gamete (with an extra chromosome) is fertilised by a normal gamete, the chromosome number in the resulting zygote is:
questions.explanation: Normal gamete has n = 12 chromosomes. Non-disjunction during Meiosis II causes sister chromatids of one pair to both go to the same cell, producing a gamete with n + 1 = 13 chromosomes. Fertilisation: 13 + 12 = 25 chromosomes (trisomy; 2n + 1 condition).

questions.text: Which of the following restriction endonucleases and their recognition sequences is INCORRECTLY matched?
questions.explanation: SmaI recognises the palindromic sequence 5'-CCCGGG-3' and cuts between the two C's to give blunt ends. It does NOT recognise 5'-GGATCC-3', which is the recognition site of BamHI. EcoRI (GAATTC), BamHI (GGATCC), and HindIII (AAGCTT) are all correctly matched.
